name: Graph Structure
desc: A graph structure represents entities (nodes) and their relationships (edges), fundamental to modeling connected data.

props.spec: connected(G) ⇔ ∀n₁, n₂ ∈ N: ∃ path from n₁ to n₂
props.d: Graph connectivity ensures every node pair has a connecting path
Acyclic graphs have no circular paths

ops.sig: path(n₁: N, n₂: N) → Sequence⟨N⟩ | null
ops.def: $path(n₁: N, n₂: N) → Sequence⟨N⟩ | null = find\_path(n₁, n₂, visited = ∅)$
$traverse(n: N, depth: ℕ) → Set⟨N⟩ = {n' ∈ N : distance(n, n') ≤ depth}$
$neighbors(n: N) → Set⟨N⟩ = {n' ∈ N : (n, n') ∈ E ∨ (n', n) ∈ E}$

name: Document/Artifact
desc: Documents and artifacts process input to produce output, encapsulating computational transformation patterns.

types.d: Input data provided to the document/artifact
Internal state with data and temporal information

props.d: Valid inputs always produce correct outputs
Processing completes within acceptable time bounds
Interface is usable by all intended users

ops.def: $\text{process}(i) = o$ where $o$ is computed result
$\text{display}(o)$ shows output to user
$\text{update}(s)$ refreshes display

name: Symbolic Expression
desc: Symbolic expressions represent mathematical and computational formulas as structured tree data.

props.d: Expressions can be serialized and parsed back to original form
All well-formed expressions evaluate to a value or error

props.spec: ∃transform: simplify(T) = T' where semantics(T) = semantics(T') ∧ complexity(T') ≤ complexity(T)
props.d: Expressions can be simplified while preserving semantics

ops.def: $parse(s: String) → Tree | ParseError = construct AST from string representation$

$eval(T: Tree,
        context: Map⟨String,Value⟩) → Value = case T.root of Literal(v) → v Variable(x) → context[x] Operator(op) → op(map(eval,
        T.children))$
$simplify(T: Tree) → Tree = apply rewrite rules until fixpoint$

name: Metadata Schema
desc: Metadata schemas define structured data fields with types and validation rules.

props.d: All field values match their declared types
All fields pass their validation rules

props.spec: extend(M, k, v) → M' where keys(M'.data) = keys(M.data) ∪ {k}
props.d: Schema can be extended with new fields

ops.sig: add(M: Metadata, k: String, v: Value) → Metadata
ops.def: $add(M: Metadata, k: String, v: Value) → Metadata = M' where M'.data[k] = v ∧ validate(M'.schema[k], v)$
$query(M: Metadata, pred: Predicate) → 𝔹 = pred(M.data)$

ops.sig: index(docs: Set⟨Document⟩, field: String) → Map⟨Value, Set⟨Document⟩⟩
ops.def: $index(docs: Set⟨Document⟩, field: String) → Map⟨Value, Set⟨Document⟩⟩ = group documents by field value$

name: Version History
desc: Version history tracks changes over time as immutable states and deltas.

props.spec: ∀s ∈ states: s is immutable once committed

props.d: Any state can be reconstructed from initial state and deltas

props.spec: branch(s, name) creates new branch from state s
props.d: History supports diverging branches from any state

ops.def: $checkout(H: History, t: Time) → State = states[max{i : states[i].timestamp ≤ t}]$

ops.sig: diff(H: History, t₁: Time, t₂: Time) → Delta
ops.def: $diff(H: History, t₁: Time, t₂: Time) → Delta = compute difference between states at t₁ and t₂$

$revert(H: History, t: Time) → History = H'
    where H'.states = H.states + [checkout(H, t)]$

name: Input Capture Flow
desc: Input capture validates and normalizes external inputs before storing in the system.

types.d: Input that passed all validation rules
Observable effect of the capture operation

props.spec: ∀i ∈ Input: store(i) ⇒ validator(i) = Valid No invalid input reaches storage
props.d: All stored inputs must pass validation

props.spec: ∀v ∈ Valid: normalizer(v) produces consistent Internal format
props.d: Validated inputs are normalized to consistent format

ops.def: $capture(input: Input) → Effect = case validator(input) of Valid(v) → store(normalizer(v)) Invalid(e) → log_error(e); reject(input)$
$validate(input: Input) → Valid | Invalid = apply\_validation\_rules(input); if passed: Valid(input) else: Invalid(errors)$
$normalize(valid: Valid) → Internal = transform_to_canonical_format(valid)$

name: Data Import Flow
desc: Data import parses external formats and transforms them to internal schema.

types.d: External data sources in various formats
Final transformed data matching internal schema
Strategy for handling conflicts during import

props.d: Parser handles all supported external formats

props.spec: ∀p ∈ ParsedData: transformer(p) conforms to internal schema
props.d: All transformed data conforms to internal schema

props.spec: ∀d ∈ InternalData: integrator(d) applies merge strategy consistently
props.d: Merge conflicts are resolved according to strategy

ops.def: $import(source: ExternalFormat, strategy: MergeStrategy) → Effect = parsed := parser(source) internal := transformer(parsed) integrator(internal, strategy)$
$parse(source: ExternalFormat) → ParsedData = case format_of(source) of JSON → parse_json(source) CSV → parse_csv(source) XML → parse_xml(source) _ → error("Unsupported format")$
$transform(parsed: ParsedData) → InternalData = map_fields(parsed, field_mapping); validate_schema(result)$

name: Live Data Stream Flow
desc: Live data stream processes continuous event flows with buffering and backpressure.

types.d: Individual event in the stream with temporal and contextual data
Consumer registered to receive stream events

props.spec: ∀e₁, e₂ ∈ Stream: timestamp(e₁) < timestamp(e₂) ⇒ process_order(e₁) < process_order(e₂)
props.d: Events are processed in timestamp order
Every processed event reaches at least one subscriber

ops.def: $stream(source: Source) → Effect = for event in source.events(): buffer.enqueue(event) if buffer.full(): apply_backpressure() process_next()$
$process(event: Event) → ProcessedEvent = validated := validate(event) enriched := enrich(validated) return enriched$
$emit(event: ProcessedEvent) → Effect = for subscriber in subscribers: subscriber.callback(event)$

name: Processing Pipeline Flow
desc: Processing pipelines compose sequential transformation stages with intermediate representations.

types.d: Individual transformation stage in the pipeline
Intermediate data format between pipeline stages
Error recovery strategies for failed stages

props.d: Pipeline stages connect in sequence with compatible types
Errors are handled or propagate to abort pipeline
Pipeline produces deterministic results for same input

ops.def: $execute(input: Input) → Output | Error = fold(stages, input, λ(acc, stage): stage.transform(acc))$
$compose(stages: Sequence⟨Stage⟩) → (Input → Output) = λ input: fold(stages, input, λ(acc, stage): stage(acc))$
$handle_error(error: Error, stage: Stage) → Recovery = case error_type(error) of Transient → Retry(stage, max_attempts=3) Permanent → Abort(error) Recoverable → Fallback(alternative_stage)$

name: Validation Flow
desc: Validation flows check data integrity and business rules at processing boundaries.

types.d: Formal schema defining expected data structure

types.def: (name: String, check: Data → 𝔹, message: String)
(id: String, compute: Inputs → Output, dependencies: Set⟨Node⟩)

props.d: All validation checks are performed before approval
Validation stops at first critical violation
All violations include actionable error messages

ops.def: $update(changed: Set⟨Node⟩) → Effect = affected := transitive_dependencies(changed) invalidate(affected) sorted := topological_sort(affected) for node in sorted: cache[node] := recompute(node)$
fx: Marks changed nodes as needing validation

ops.def: $invalidate(nodes: Set⟨Node⟩) → Set⟨Node⟩ = invalidated := nodes for node in nodes: for dependent in dependents(node): invalidated := invalidated ∪ invalidate({dependent}) return invalidated$
$recompute(node: Node) → Value = inputs := {cache[d] : d ∈ dependencies(node)} return node.compute(inputs)$

ops.sig: validate_schema(data: Data, schema: Schema) → Valid | Invalid
ops.def: $validate\_schema(data: Data, schema: Schema) → Valid | Invalid = check\_types(data, schema); check\_constraints(data, schema); if passed: Valid(data) else: Invalid(violations)$
$validate\_rules(data: Data, rules: Set⟨Rule⟩) → Set⟨Violation⟩ = {v : rule ∈ rules, v = rule.check(data), ¬v.passed}$
$report(violations: Set⟨Violation⟩) → Report = format\_violations(violations); categorize\_by\_severity()$

name: Enrichment Flow
desc: Enrichment flows augment data with additional context from external sources.

props.d: All configured enrichment sources are queried
Enrichment continues even if some sources fail
Original data fields are preserved in enriched result

ops.def: $enrich(data: Data, sources: Set⟨Source⟩) → EnrichedData = enrichments := fetch\_enrichments(data, sources); merge(data, enrichments)$
$fetch\_enrichment(data: Data, source: EnrichmentSource) → EnrichmentData = source.query(extract\_keys(data))$
$merge(base: Data, enrichment: EnrichmentData) → EnrichedData = base ∪ enrichment with conflict\_resolution$
$combine(base: BaseData, enrichments: Set⟨Enrichment⟩) → EnrichedData = result := base for enrichment in enrichments: result := merge(result, enrichment) return result$

name: State Transition Flow
desc: State transition flows manage discrete state changes through defined transitions.

types.d: One of the defined system states

props.spec: ∀s, e: transition(s, e) produces same result

props.d: All non-terminal states have at least one valid transition

ops.def: $loop(environment: Environment) → Effect = observations := [] model := initialize_model() while running: obs := observe(environment) observations := observations + [obs] if should_update(observations): model := learn(observations) policy := update(model) actions := apply(policy, environment)$
$learn(observations: Observations) → Model = features := extract_features(observations) model := train(features, current_model) validate(model, validation_set) return model$
$apply(policy: Policy, env: Environment) → Actions = state := env.current_state() action := policy.select_action(state) env.execute(action) return action$

ops.sig: transition(current: State, event: Event) → State | Error
ops.def: $transition(current: State, event: Event) → State | Error = if allowed(current, event): next := rules[current][event]; execute\_actions(current, event, next); return next else: return Error("Invalid transition")$
$allowed(from: State, event: Event) → 𝔹 = (from, event) ∈ valid\_transitions$
$valid\_events(state: State) → Set⟨Event⟩ = {e : allowed(state, e)}$

name: Event-Driven Flow
desc: Event-driven flows react to events by dispatching handlers and updating state.

types.d: Event with type, payload, and timestamp

types.def: (id: String, check: Data → 𝔹, message: String)

props.d: Event emitters are decoupled from handlers
Handler failures don't affect other handlers

ops.def: $execute(data: Data) → Report = violations := validate(data) if empty(violations): return Success else: for v in violations: report(v) if auto_correctable(v): correction := correct(v) if verify(correction): apply(correction)$
fx: Runs all validation rules on data

ops.def: $validate(data: Data) → Set⟨Violation⟩ = violations := {} for rule in rules: if ¬rule.check(data): violations := violations ∪ {Violation(rule, location(data), severity(rule))} return violations$
fx: Returns empty set if data valid

ops.def: $correct(violation: Violation) → Correction = if has_automatic_fix(violation): return Automatic(generate_fix(violation)) else: return Manual(suggest_fix(violation))$
$emit(event: Event) → Effect = listeners := get\_listeners(event.type); for listener in listeners: dispatch(listener, event)$
$subscribe(event\_type: EventType, handler: Handler) → Subscription = listeners[event\_type] := listeners[event\_type] ∪ {handler}; return Subscription(id, unsubscribe)$
$handle(event: Event, handler: Handler) → Effect = try: handler.process(event) catch error: log\_error(error); retry\_or\_skip()$

name: Adaptation Loop Flow
desc: Adaptation loops monitor system behavior and adjust parameters dynamically.

types.d: System property that can be measured

types.def: (metric: String, threshold: ℝ, actual: ℝ, severity: ℕ)

props.d: System converges to goals over time
Adaptations are bounded to prevent instability
Each adaptation improves or maintains performance

ops.def: $monitor(metrics: Set⟨Metric⟩) → Observations = collect\_current\_values(metrics); analyze\_trends()$
$analyze(obs: Observations, goals: Goals) → Analysis = deviations := compare(obs, goals); diagnose\_issues(deviations)$
$adapt(analysis: Analysis) → Effect = adjustments := compute\_adjustments(analysis); apply\_safely(adjustments)$
Execute F3.3 operation

name: Feedback Loop Flow
desc: Feedback loops use output to adjust input in closed-loop control systems.

types.def: Click | View | Purchase | Search | Rate | Configure
(user: UserId, action: UserAction, context: Context, timestamp: Time)

ops.def: $personalize(user: UserId) → Effect = events := track(user) preferences := infer(events) config := personalize(preferences, user.explicit_settings) apply(config, user.session)$
fx: Customizes user experience based on behavior

ops.def: $infer(events: Sequence⟨Event⟩) → Preferences = features := extract_features(events) patterns := find_patterns(features) preferences := {} for (feature, pattern) in patterns: score := calculate_affinity(pattern) preferences[feature] := score return preferences$

$apply(config: Configuration, session: Session) → Effect = for (setting, value) in config: session.set(setting, value) content := filter_by_preferences(available_content, config) session.recommendations := rank(content, config)$
$sense() → Measurement = read\_sensors(); calibrate\_reading()$
$compare(measurement: Measurement, setpoint: Setpoint) → Error = setpoint - measurement$
$actuate(control: ControlSignal) → Effect = clamp(control, limits); apply\_to\_system(control)$

name: Presentation Flow
desc: Presentation flows prepare and format data for display to users.

types.def: Set⟨Operation⟩ where Operation := Insert | Update | Delete | Move

props.d: Data and presentation logic are separated
Content can be presented in any supported locale
Presentation adapts to different display sizes

ops.def: $update(new_state: State) → Effect = old_vdom := render(current_state) new_vdom := render(new_state) patch := diff(old_vdom, new_vdom) apply_patch(patch, real_dom) current_state := new_state$

$diff(old: VirtualDOM, new: VirtualDOM) → Patch = if old = new: return [] if different_type(old, new): return [Replace(old, new)] if different_props(old, new): ops := [UpdateProps(old, new)] else: ops := [] ops := ops + diff_children(old.children, new.children) return ops$

$apply_patch(patch: Patch, dom: RealDOM) → Effect = for op in patch: case op of Insert(node, parent, index) → parent.insert_child(node, index) Update(node, props) → node.update_properties(props) Delete(node) → node.remove() Move(node, new_parent, new_index) → move(node, new_parent, new_index)$
$format(data: Data, template: Template) → Presentation = bind\_data(template, data); apply\_formatting(); render()$
$localize(content: Content, locale: Locale) → Content = translate(content, locale); format\_numbers(locale); format\_dates(locale)$
fx: Formats numbers and dates for locale

ops.def: $paginate(items: Sequence⟨Item⟩, page\_size: ℕ) → Sequence⟨Page⟩ = chunk(items, page\_size)$

name: Query Flow
desc: Query flows retrieve and filter data based on criteria and return results.

ops.def: $read_model(query: Query) → Result = parse(query) optimized := optimize(parse_tree) execute(optimized, read_db) format(results)$
$project(data: Set⟨Record⟩, fields: Set⟨Field⟩) → Set⟨Record⟩ = {r' | r ∈ data, r' = select_fields(r, fields)}$

ops.sig: aggregate(data: Set⟨Record⟩, group_by: Set⟨Field⟩, agg_fn: AggFn) → Set⟨Record⟩
ops.def: $aggregate(data: Set⟨Record⟩, group_by: Set⟨Field⟩, agg_fn: AggFn) → Set⟨Record⟩ = groups := group(data, group_by) {apply(agg_fn, g) | g ∈ groups}$

name: Export and Publishing Flow
desc: Export flows transform internal data to external formats and publish to destinations.

types.def: JSON | XML | CSV | PDF | HTML | Binary

props.d: Exported data conforms to format specifications

props.spec: ∀d ∈ Data: publish(d) succeeds completely ∨ rolls back completely
props.d: Publishing is atomic - all or nothing

ops.sig: export(data: InternalData, format: ExternalFormat, dest: Destination) → PublishResult
ops.def: $export(data: InternalData, format: ExternalFormat, dest: Destination) → PublishResult = transformed := transform(data, format); validated := validate(transformed); if validated: publish(transformed, dest) else: error("Invalid format")$
$transform(data: InternalData, format: ExternalFormat) → ExternalFormat = case format of JSON → to_json(data) XML → to_xml(data) CSV → to_csv(data) PDF → to_pdf(data) HTML → to_html(data)$

$publish(data: ExternalFormat, dest: Destination) → PublishResult = case dest of File(path) → write_file(path, data) return Success(file_url(path)) URL(endpoint) → response := http_post(endpoint, data) return Success(response.location) API(service) → id := service.create(data) return Success(service.url(id)) Storage(bucket) → key := bucket.put(data) return Success(bucket.url(key))$

name: Synchronization Flow
desc: Synchronization flows keep multiple data sources consistent through coordination.

types.def: (select: Projection, from: Source, where: Predicate, aggregate: Function)

props.d: Sources eventually converge to consistent state
Repeated synchronization has no additional effect

ops.def: $publish(message: Message, channel: Channel) → Effect = serialize(message) send_to_subscribers(channel.subscribers, message) log_event(channel, message)$
$subscribe(subscriber: Subscriber, channel: Channel) → Effect = channel.subscribers := channel.subscribers ∪ {subscriber} send_confirmation(subscriber)$
$sync(source: DataSource, target: DataSource) → Effect = delta := diff(source, target) apply_changes(target, delta) verify_consistency(source, target)$

name: Collaborative Editing Flow
desc: Collaborative editing enables multiple users to edit shared documents concurrently with conflict resolution.

props.d: All users eventually see the same document

ops.def: $edit(user: User, op: Operation) → Effect = D_user := apply(op, D_user) broadcast(op, user)$
$receive(op: Operation, u_remote: User) → Effect = op' := transform(op, local_ops) D := apply(op', D)$
$transform(op1: Operation, op2: Operation) → Operation = adjust op1 for concurrent effects of op2; resolve conflicts using deterministic rules$

name: Error Recovery Flow
desc: Error recovery flows detect failures and apply strategies to restore normal operation.

types.def: Retry(max: ℕ, backoff: Time → Time) | Fallback(alternative: Operation) | Abort

props.spec: ∀e ∈ Error: classify(e) determines appropriate recovery strategy Transient → Retry Permanent → Report Recoverable → Fallback
props.d: Errors are classified to select appropriate recovery

props.spec: ∀e ∈ Error: recover(e) preserves system invariants No partial state corruption

ops.sig: retry(op: Operation, max: ℕ, backoff: Time → Time) → Result | Error
ops.def: $retry(op: Operation, max: ℕ, backoff: Time → Time) → Result | Error = for attempt in 1..max: try: return op(); catch error: wait(backoff(attempt)); throw MaxAttemptsExceeded$

ops.sig: circuit_breaker(op: Operation, threshold: ℕ) → Result | Error
ops.def: $circuit\_breaker(op: Operation, threshold: ℕ) → Result | Error = if failures > threshold: return CircuitOpen; try: result := op(); reset\_failures(); return result; catch: increment\_failures(); throw$

ops.sig: degrade(levels: Sequence⟨Mode⟩, operation: Mode → Result) → Result
ops.def: $degrade(levels: Sequence⟨Mode⟩, operation: Mode → Result) → Result = for mode in levels: try: return operation(mode) catch error: continue throw AllModesFailedError$

name: Direct Manipulation Canvas
desc: Direct manipulation canvases enable users to interact with visual objects through direct gestures.

props.d: Elements snap to guides when nearby

ops.def: $select(point: Point) → Element | null = elements := hit\_test(point); if |elements| > 0: return topmost(elements) else: return null$
$drag(element: Element, delta: Vector) → Effect = element.position := element.position + delta; update\_connections(element); redraw()$

ops.sig: resize(element: Element, handle: Handle, delta: Vector) → Effect
ops.def: $resize(element: Element, handle: Handle, delta: Vector) → Effect = new\_bounds := compute\_bounds(element, handle, delta); element.bounds := new\_bounds; maintain\_aspect\_ratio(); redraw()$

$interpret(gesture: Gesture) → Action = case gesture of Click(p) → Select(objectAt(p)) Drag(p₁, p₂) → Move(selected, p₂ - p₁) Scroll(Δ) → Pan(viewport, Δ) persist(objects: Set⟨Element⟩) → Effect = save_to_storage(objects); notify_observers(objects)$

name: Parser/Compiler Pipeline
desc: Parser/compiler pipelines transform source code through multiple stages to produce executable output.

props.d: Errors include location and helpful messages

ops.def: $process(input: Input) → Output = validated := validate(input) transformed := transform(validated) optimized := optimize(transformed) return optimized$
$display(data: Data) → Effect = formatted := format(data) rendered := render(formatted) update_view(rendered)$
$update(changes: Set⟨Change⟩) → Effect = for change in changes: apply(change) notify_observers(change)$
$scan(source: String) → Sequence⟨Token⟩ = tokens := [] pos := 0 while pos < |source|: (token, new_pos) := next_token(source, pos) tokens := tokens + [token] pos := new_pos return tokens$
$parse(tokens: Sequence⟨Token⟩) → AST = ast := build_tree(tokens, grammar) validate_syntax(ast) return ast$
$optimize(ast: AST) → AST = folded := constant_folding(ast) inlined := inline_functions(folded) dce := dead_code_elimination(inlined) return dce$
$lex(source: String) → Sequence⟨Token⟩ = tokenize(source); classify\_tokens()$
$compile(ast: AST) → Code | CompileError = type\_check(ast); optimize(ast); generate\_code()$

name: Validator/Checker
desc: Validators check data against rules and constraints to ensure correctness and completeness.

types.def: (name: String, check: Data → 𝔹, message: String)

props.d: All rules are checked during validation
Error messages are clear and actionable

ops.sig: Type mismatch: expected T1, got T2
ops.def: $type\_error(expected: Type, actual: Type) → Error = create\_error("Type mismatch: expected " + expected + ", got " + actual, location)$
$semantic\_error(kind: ErrorKind, context: Context) → Error = create\_error(error\_message(kind), context.location)$
$syntax\_error(token: Token, expected: Set⟨Token⟩) → Error = create\_error("Unexpected " + token + ", expected one of " + expected, token.location)$

name: Solver/Optimizer
desc: Solvers and optimizers find optimal solutions to constrained problems using algorithms.

ops.def: $process(input: Input) → Output = validated := validate(input) transformed := transform(validated) result := compute(transformed) return result$
$display(data: Data) → Effect = formatted := format(data) rendered := render(formatted) show(rendered)$
$update(changes: Set⟨Change⟩) → Effect = for c in changes: apply(c) invalidate\_cache() notify(c)$
$solve(problem: Problem) → Solution = constraints := extract_constraints(problem) initial := initialize_solution() optimized := optimize(initial, constraints) return optimized$
$optimize(solution: Solution, objective: Objective) → Solution = improved := solution while can_improve(improved, objective): improved := improve_step(improved, objective) return improved$
$verify(solution: Solution, problem: Problem) → 𝔹 = ∀c ∈ problem.constraints: satisfies(solution, c)$
$verify(solution: Solution, constraints: Set⟨Constraint⟩) → 𝔹 = ∀c ∈ constraints: c.check(solution)$

name: Indexer/Query Engine
desc: Indexers build searchable indexes and query engines retrieve relevant information efficiently.

ops.def: $process(input: Input) → Output = parsed := parse(input) indexed := index(parsed) return indexed$
$display(data: Data) → Effect = formatted := format(data) render(formatted)$
$update(changes: Set⟨Change⟩) → Effect = for c in changes: update\_index(c) refresh\_cache()$
$index(doc: Document) → Effect = terms := extract\_terms(doc); for term in terms: inverted\_index[term] := inverted\_index[term] ∪ {doc.id}; store\_metadata(doc)$
$search(query: Query) → ResultSet = terms := parse(query); doc\_ids := intersect({inverted\_index[t] : t ∈ terms}); docs := fetch(doc\_ids); ranked := rank(docs, query); return ResultSet(ranked)$
$rebuild() → Effect = clear\_index(); for doc in all\_documents: index(doc)$

name: Agent Swarm
desc: Agent swarms coordinate multiple AI agents to solve complex problems collaboratively.

types.def: (id: ID, status: Status, execute: Task → Result)

ops.def: $enqueue(agent: Agent, task: Task) → Effect = queue(agent) := queue(agent) + [task]$
fx: Adds task to agent's work queue

ops.sig: enqueue_priority(agent: Agent, task: Task, priority: ℕ) → Effect
ops.def: $enqueue\_priority(agent: Agent, task: Task, priority: ℕ) → Effect = queue(agent) := insert\_sorted(queue(agent), task, priority)$
fx: Adds task with priority to queue

ops.def: $steal_work(idle\_agent: Agent) → Task | null = busy\_agents := filter(agents, λa: |queue(a)| > 1); if empty(busy\_agents): return null; victim := max(busy\_agents, λa: |queue(a)|); task := pop(queue(victim)); return task$
fx: Idle agents take work from busy ones

ops.def: $dispatch(task: Task) → Effect = tasks := enqueue(tasks, task, task.priority) if ∃agent: agent.status = Idle: assign(agent, task)$
$assign(agent: Agent, task: Task) → Effect = agent.status := Busy(task) result := agent.execute(task) results[task] := result agent.status := Idle try_assign_next(agent)$

$deploy(task: Task, size: ℕ) → Swarm = agents := {create\_agent(i, task) : i ∈ 1..size}; coordinator := create\_coordinator(agents); swarm := Swarm(agents, coordinator); initialize\_swarm(swarm); return swarm$

$coordinate(swarm: Swarm, subtasks: Set⟨SubTask⟩) → Effect = assignments := assign\_tasks(swarm.agents, subtasks); for (agent, task) in assignments: dispatch(agent, task); monitor\_progress(assignments)$
$aggregate(swarm: Swarm) → Result = results := collect\_agent\_results(swarm.agents); merged := merge\_results(results); validated := validate\_consistency(merged); return validated$

name: Reasoning Chain
desc: Reasoning chains show step-by-step logical inference and decision-making processes.

types.def: (id: ℕ, operation: Operation, inputs: Set⟨ℕ⟩, output: Value)

ops.def: $display(data: Data) → Effect = formatted := format(data) render(formatted)$

name: Suggestion/Recommendation System
desc: Suggestion and recommendation systems analyze context to provide relevant suggestions to users.

ops.def: $process(user: User) → Suggestions = profile := get\_profile(user) candidates := generate\_candidates(profile) ranked := rank(candidates, profile) return top\_k(ranked, k)$
$display(suggestions: Suggestions) → Effect = formatted := format(suggestions) render(formatted)$
$update(feedback: Feedback) → Effect = model := get\_model() updated := train(model, feedback) save(updated)$

$suggest(context: Context, history: UserHistory) → Set⟨Suggestion⟩ = candidates := generate\_candidates(context); preferences := infer\_preferences(history); scored := {(s, score(s, preferences, context)) : s ∈ candidates}; return top\_k(scored, k)$

$apply(layout: Layout) → Effect = structure := layout.structure for (element, visible) in layout.visibility: element.visible := visible reflow()$

$register(bp: Breakpoint, layout: Layout) → Effect = breakpoints := insert_sorted(breakpoints, bp) layouts[bp.name] := layout mobile: < 640px tablet: 640px - 1024px desktop: 1024px - 1440px wide: ≥ 1440px$
$detect() → ViewportSize = width := window.innerWidth; height := window.innerHeight; return ViewportSize(width, height)$
$apply(viewport: ViewportSize) → Layout = breakpoint := find\_breakpoint(viewport.width); layout := layouts[breakpoint]; apply\_layout(layout); return layout$
$reflow() → Effect = viewport := detect(); new\_layout := apply(viewport); if new\_layout ≠ current\_layout: animate\_transition(); render(new\_layout)$

name: Pagination Pattern
desc: Pagination pattern divides large datasets into manageable pages for display.

ops.def: $paginate\_offset(page: ℕ, size: ℕ) → (ℕ, ℕ) = offset := (page - 1) × size; (offset, size)$
fx: Calculates SQL OFFSET and LIMIT values

ops.sig: paginate_cursor(cursor: String | null, size: ℕ) → (Cursor, ℕ)
ops.def: $paginate\_cursor(cursor: String | null, size: ℕ) → (Cursor, ℕ) = start := decode(cursor); (start, size)$
fx: Returns next page starting from cursor

ops.def: $numbered\_pages(current: ℕ, total: ℕ) → Sequence⟨ℕ⟩ = [1, ..., current-1, current, current+1, ..., total]$
fx: Generates page number sequence for UI
Shows ellipsis for large page counts

ops.def: $simple\_nav() → (Button, Button) = (Previous, Next)$
$load\_more(loaded: ℕ, increment: ℕ) → ℕ = loaded + increment$
fx: Appends next batch to existing list

ops.def:
$get_page(page_num: ℕ) → Sequence⟨Item⟩ = start := (page_num - 1) · size end := min(start + size,
        total) return data[start:end]$
fx: Returns empty if page beyond range

ops.def: $set_page_size(new_size: ℕ) → Effect = size := new_size page := 1 // Reset to first page load_page(1)$

ops.sig: Scroll to bottom → load_next_page() → append to list
ops.def: Scroll to bottom → load_next_page() → append to list Pros: Smooth UX Cons: No page bookmarks, harder navigation
$goto(page\_num: ℕ) → Page = if valid\_page(page\_num): offset := (page\_num - 1) × page\_size; items := fetch\_items(offset, page\_size); current\_page := page\_num; return Page(items, page\_num)$
$next() → Page = if current\_page < total\_pages: return goto(current\_page + 1)$
$set\_size(size: ℕ) → Effect = page\_size := size; total\_pages := ⌈total\_items / size⌉; goto(1)$

name: Filtering Pattern
desc: Filtering pattern enables users to narrow down displayed data based on criteria.

props.d: Multiple filters combine with AND logic

ops.def: Price: $0 - $1000; apply(item, (min, max)) = min ≤ item.price ≤ max; Search: \"laptop\"; apply(item, query) = contains(item.name, query, case_insensitive)
[✓] In Stock Only; apply(item, true) = item.in_stock

$apply_filter(filter_name: String, value: FilterValue) → Effect = active[filter_name] := value result := compute_result(data, active) facets := compute_facets(result, filters) notify_observers(result)$

ops.sig: compute_result(data: Set⟨Item⟩, active: Map⟨String, FilterValue⟩) → Set⟨Item⟩
ops.def: $compute_result(data: Set⟨Item⟩, active: Map⟨String, FilterValue⟩) → Set⟨Item⟩ = filter(data, λitem: ∀(k, v) ∈ active: filters[k].apply(item, v) )$

ops.sig: compute_facets(result: Set⟨Item⟩, filters: Map⟨String, Filter⟩) → Map⟨String, Facet⟩
ops.def: $compute_facets(result: Set⟨Item⟩, filters: Map⟨String, Filter⟩) → Map⟨String, Facet⟩ = { (filter_name, { (value, count(result, λitem: item[filter_name] = value)) for value in possible_values(filter_name) }) for filter_name in keys(filters) }$
fx: Shows available filter options with counts

ops.def: $clear() → Effect = active := {} result := data facets := compute_facets(result, filters)$
$add(filter: Filter) → Effect = active\_filters := active\_filters ∪ {filter}; apply\_filters()$
$remove(filter: Filter) → Effect = active\_filters := active\_filters \ {filter}; apply\_filters()$
$apply() → Set⟨Item⟩ = result := all\_items; for filter in active\_filters: result := {item ∈ result : filter.matches(item)}; return result$

name: Sorting Pattern
desc: Sorting pattern enables users to order data by specified criteria.

types.def: (name: String, type: Type, extract: Item → Value)

ops.sig: reorder(items: Sequence⟨Item⟩, from: ℕ, to: ℕ) → Sequence⟨Item⟩
ops.def: $reorder(items: Sequence⟨Item⟩, from: ℕ, to: ℕ) → Sequence⟨Item⟩ = item := items[from]; remove(items, from); insert(items, to, item)$
fx: Moves item from position to new position

ops.def: $sort(data: Sequence⟨Item⟩, order: Order) → Sequence⟨Item⟩ = stable_sort(data, composite_comparator(order))$

$composite_comparator(order: Order) → Comparator = λ(item1, item2): for (column, direction) in order: val1 := column.extract(item1) val2 := column.extract(item2) cmp := compare(val1, val2, column.type) if cmp ≠ 0: return cmp · (direction = Asc ? 1 : -1) return 0 // All keys equal$
fx: Falls back to next column on equality

ops.def: $toggle_sort(column: Column) → Effect = if order[0].column = column: order[0].direction := flip(order[0].direction) else: order := [(column, Asc)] ++ order data := sort(data, order)$
$clear_sort() → Effect = order := [] data := original_data$

ops.sig: Click column header → sort by that column
ops.def: Click column header → sort by that column Click again → reverse direction
Click column → primary sort Shift+Click another → secondary sort Shift+Click third → tertiary sort
$sort(field: Field, direction: Direction) → Sequence⟨Item⟩ = comparator := get\_comparator(field, direction); sorted := sort\_items(items, comparator); return sorted$
$toggle(field: Field) → Effect = if current\_sort.field = field: current\_sort.direction := invert(current\_sort.direction) else: current\_sort := (field, ASC); apply\_sort()$
$multi\_sort(criteria: Sequence⟨(Field, Direction)⟩) → Sequence⟨Item⟩ = comparator := compose\_comparators(criteria); sorted := sort\_items(items, comparator); return sorted$

name: Virtualization Pattern
desc: Virtualization pattern renders only visible items for efficient handling of large lists.

comps.d: number of items to pre-render beyond viewport

ops.def: $virtualize\_variable(items: Sequence⟨Item⟩, viewport: Viewport) → Sequence⟨Item⟩ = calculate heights; find visible range; render visible$
fx: Renders only visible items with varying heights
Dynamically adjusts based on measured heights

ops.def: $estimate\_height(item: Item) → ℕ = default\_height + estimate\_content(item)$
$measure\_height(rendered: Element) → ℕ = rendered.getBoundingClientRect().height$

$calculate_visible(scroll_top: ℝ) → (start: ℕ, end: ℕ) = start_idx := binary_search(data, λi: cumulative_height(data[0:i]) ≥ scroll_top - buffer_height ) end_idx := binary_search(data, λi: cumulative_height(data[0:i]) ≥ scroll_top + viewport.height + buffer_height ) return (start_idx, end_idx)$
fx: Based on scroll position and item heights

ops.def: $on_scroll(scroll_top: ℝ) → Effect = (start, end) := calculate_visible(scroll_top) visible := data[start:end] offset := cumulative_height(data[0:start]) render(visible, offset)$

$visible() → (ℕ, ℕ) = scroll\_top := viewport.scrollTop; start\_index := ⌊scroll\_top / item\_height⌋; visible\_count := ⌈viewport.height / item\_height⌉; end\_index := start\_index + visible\_count + buffer; return (start\_index, end\_index)$
$render(start: ℕ, end: ℕ) → Effect = items\_to\_render := items[start:end]; render\_items(items\_to\_render); update\_spacers(start, end)$
$scroll() → Effect = (start, end) := visible(); if range\_changed(start, end): render(start, end)$

name: Cache Strategy Pattern
desc: Cache strategy pattern stores frequently accessed data for faster retrieval.

ops.def: $cache\_aside(key: Key) → Value = if key ∈ cache: return cache[key] else: val := db.get(key); cache[key] := val; return val$
fx: Loads from DB on miss and populates cache

ops.def: $read\_through(key: Key) → Value = cache.get(key) // Cache loads from DB automatically$
fx: Cache transparently loads data on miss

ops.def: $write\_through(key: Key, value: Value) → Effect = db.set(key, value); cache.set(key, value)$
fx: Writes to cache and DB synchronously

ops.def: $write\_behind(key: Key, value: Value) → Effect = cache.set(key, value); queue\_db\_write(key, value)$
fx: Queues DB write for async processing

ops.def: $get(key: Key, load: () → Value) → Value = if key ∈ storage ∧ ¬expired(key): update_metadata(key) return storage[key].value else: value := load() set(key, value) return value$
fx: Loads and caches on miss using provided loader

ops.def: $set(key: Key, value: Value) → Effect = if |storage| = max_size: evict() storage[key] := (value, Metadata(now(), 0, now()))$
$get(key: Key) → Value | null = if key ∈ cache ∧ ¬expired(cache[key]): update\_access\_time(key); return cache[key].value else: return null$

ops.sig: put(key: Key, value: Value, ttl: Time) → Effect
ops.def: $put(key: Key, value: Value, ttl: Time) → Effect = if |cache| ≥ max\_size: evict(); cache[key] := CacheEntry(value, expires=now()+ttl, accessed=now())$
$evict() → Effect = victim := select\_victim(eviction\_policy); cache := cache \ {victim}$

name: Data Migration Pattern
desc: Data migration pattern transforms and moves data between schema versions or systems.

props.d: Failures can be rolled back safely

ops.def: $add(state: State, item: Item) → State = state' where state'.items = state.items ∪ {item}$
fx: Adds new item without modifying existing data

ops.sig: transform(state: State, f: State → State) → State
ops.def: $transform(state: State, f: State → State) → State = f(state)$
$reset() → State = initial\_state; // Warning: loses all data$
$migrate_up(target: Version) → Effect = while current < target: migration := migrations[(current, next(current))] data := migration.up(data) current := next(current)$
fx: Applies migrations sequentially to target version

ops.def: $migrate_down(target: Version) → Effect = while current > target: migration := migrations[(prev(current), current)] data := migration.down(data) current := prev(current)$
fx: Rolls back migrations to target version

ops.sig: create_migration(from: Version, to: Version, up: Transform, down: Transform) → Migration
ops.def: $create_migration(from: Version, to: Version, up: Transform, down: Transform) → Migration = Migration(up, down)$
$plan(from: Schema, to: Schema) → MigrationPlan = diff := compute\_schema\_diff(from, to); steps := generate\_migration\_steps(diff); validate\_plan(steps); return MigrationPlan(steps)$

$execute(plan: MigrationPlan) → MigrationResult = backup := create\_backup(); for step in plan.steps: try: execute\_step(step); log\_success(step) catch error: rollback(backup); return Failure(error); return Success(migrated\_count)$
$rollback(backup: Backup) → Effect = restore\_from\_backup(backup); log\_rollback()$

name: Authentication Pattern
desc: Batch processing pattern handles large-scale operations in manageable chunks.

types.def: (id: UserID, username: String, email: String, roles: Set⟨Role⟩)
(id: SessionID, user: UserID, created: Time, expires: Time, data: Map⟨String,Value⟩)

ops.def: $authenticate(credentials: Credentials) → User | null = user := find\_user(credentials.username); if user ∧ verify\_password(credentials.password, user.hash): return user; else: return null$
fx: Returns user on success, null on failure

ops.sig: register(username: String, password: String) → User | Error
ops.def: $register(username: String, password: String) → User | Error = if exists(username): return Error(\"User exists\"); hash := hash\_password(password); user := create\_user(username, hash); return user$
$verify\_password(plain: String, hash: String) → 𝔹 = hash(plain + salt) = hash$
$create\_session(user: User) → Session = session := Session(id: uuid(), user: user.id, expires: now() + TTL); store\_session(session); return session$
$validate\_session(session\_id: String) → User | null = session := get\_session(session\_id); if session ∧ session.expires > now(): return get\_user(session.user); else: return null$
$generate\_token(user: User) → String = payload := {user\_id: user.id, exp: now() + TTL}; sign(payload, secret\_key)$
fx: Creates signed JWT with user claims

ops.def: $session\_auth() → AuthStrategy = server stores session state; cookie contains session ID$
$token\_auth() → AuthStrategy = stateless; token contains all auth info$
$oauth2(provider: OAuthProvider) → Token = redirect\_to\_provider(provider); receive\_auth\_code(); exchange\_code\_for\_token()$
$verify\_mfa(user: User, code: String) → 𝔹 = totp := generate\_totp(user.secret, now()); code = totp$
$create(items: Sequence⟨Item⟩, batch\_size: ℕ) → Batch = chunks := partition(items, batch\_size); batch := Batch(id=generate\_id(), chunks, status=Pending); return batch$

$process(batch: Batch, processor: Processor) → BatchResult = batch.status := Processing; results := ∅; for chunk in batch.chunks: try: result := processor(chunk); results := results ∪ {result}; update\_progress() catch error: handle\_error(error); batch.status := Complete; return BatchResult(results)$
$retry(batch: Batch) → Effect = failed\_chunks := {c : c ∈ batch.chunks, c.status = Failed}; batch.chunks := failed\_chunks; batch.status := Pending; process(batch)$

name: Authorization Pattern
desc: Rate limiting pattern controls request frequency to prevent overload.

types.def: Resource(id: ID, type: Type, owner: Subject)
Read | Write | Execute | Delete | Share | Admin

ops.sig: rbac_check(user: User, resource: Resource, action: Action) → 𝔹
ops.def: $rbac\_check(user: User, resource: Resource, action: Action) → 𝔹 = roles := get\_roles(user); permissions := flatten(map(get\_permissions, roles)); has\_permission(permissions, resource, action)$
post: result = true \iff \exists r \in user.roles: permits(r, resource, action)
fx: Checks if user role allows action on resource

ops.sig: abac_check(subject: Subject, resource: Resource, action: Action, env: Environment) → 𝔹
ops.def: $abac\_check(subject: Subject, resource: Resource, action: Action, env: Environment) → 𝔹 = policies := get\_policies(); any(policies, λp: p.evaluate(subject, resource, action, env))$

ops.sig: acl_check(user: User, resource: Resource, action: Action) → 𝔹
ops.def: $acl\_check(user: User, resource: Resource, action: Action) → 𝔹 = acl := get\_acl(resource); entries := filter(acl, λe: e.principal = user); any(entries, λe: action ∈ e.permissions)$
$check\_owner(user: User, resource: Resource) → 𝔹 = resource.owner = user.id$

ops.sig: evaluate(subject: Subject, resource: Resource, action: Action) → 𝔹
ops.def: $evaluate(subject: Subject, resource: Resource, action: Action) → 𝔹 = policies := get\_policies(resource); any(policies, λp: p.allows(subject, action))$

ops.sig: check(subject: Subject, object: Object, action: Action) → 𝔹
ops.def: $check(subject: Subject, object: Object, action: Action) → 𝔹 = policies_applicable := filter(policies, λp: p.applies(subject, object, action)) // Check for explicit deny if ∃p ∈ policies_applicable: p.effect = Deny: return false // Check for explicit allow if ∃p ∈ policies_applicable: p.effect = Allow: return true // Default deny return false$

ops.sig: grant(subject: Subject, object: Object, actions: Set⟨Action⟩) → Effect
ops.def: $grant(subject: Subject, object: Object, actions: Set⟨Action⟩) → Effect = permission := Permission(subject, object, actions) permissions := permissions ∪ {permission}$
$revoke(subject: Subject, object: Object) → Effect = permissions := permissions ∖ {p : p.subject = subject ∧ p.object = object}$

$allow(client: ClientID, timestamp: Time) → 𝔹 = requests := get\_recent\_requests(client, timestamp - window); if |requests| < limit: record\_request(client, timestamp); return true else: return false$
$reset(client: ClientID) → Effect = remove\_requests(client)$

$info(client: ClientID) → LimitInfo = requests := get\_recent\_requests(client, now() - window); remaining := max(0, limit - |requests|); reset\_time := calculate\_reset\_time(requests); return LimitInfo(remaining, reset\_time)$

name: Palette/Toolbar
desc: Contextual menus provide actions relevant to the current selection or location.

ops.def: $show(position: Point, context: Context) → Menu = actions := get\_contextual\_actions(context); menu := Menu(actions, position); adjust\_position\_for\_viewport(menu); display(menu); return menu$
$execute(action: Action, context: Context) → Effect = if enabled(action, context): close\_menu(); action.handler(context)$
$close() → Effect = hide(menu); remove(menu)$
$\text{process}(i) = o$ where $o$ is computed result
$\text{display}(o)$ shows output to user
$\text{update}(s)$ refreshes display

name: Input Sanitization Pattern
desc: Retry policy pattern handles transient failures with configurable retry strategies.

types.def: Exponential(base: Time) | Linear(interval: Time) | Fixed(delay: Time)

ops.sig: validate(input: Input, schema: Schema) → Valid | Invalid
ops.def: $validate(input: Input, schema: Schema) → Valid | Invalid = check\_type(input, schema.type); check\_constraints(input, schema.rules); if passed: Valid(input) else: Invalid(errors)$
$sanitize(input: Input, rules: SanitizeRules) → Input = escape\_html(input); remove\_dangerous\_chars(input, rules); normalize(input)$
$prevent\_xss(input: String) → String = escape\_html\_entities(input); sanitize\_attributes(); content\_security\_policy()$
fx: Prevents script injection in web pages

ops.def: $prevent\_sql\_injection(query: String, params: Map) → PreparedStatement = use\_prepared\_statement(query); bind\_parameters(params); escape\_literals()$
$prevent\_path\_traversal(path: String) → String | Error = normalized := normalize\_path(path); if contains(normalized, \"..\"): return Error(\"Invalid path\"); validate\_in\_whitelist(normalized)$
$prevent\_command\_injection(cmd: String, args: Array⟨String⟩) → SafeCommand = whitelist\_command(cmd); escape\_shell\_args(args); use\_parameterized\_execution()$
$prevent\_ldap\_injection(filter: String) → String = escape\_ldap\_special\_chars(filter); validate\_filter\_syntax()$
$encode(input: Input, context: Context) → String = encoder := encoders[context] return encoder(input)$
pre: input \neq null \wedge context \in \{HTML, JS, URL, CSS\}

ops.def: $execute(operation: Operation, policy: RetryPolicy) → Result = attempt := 0; while attempt < policy.max\_attempts: try: result := operation(); return Success(result) catch error: if retryable(error) ∧ attempt < policy.max\_attempts - 1: wait(policy.backoff(attempt)); attempt := attempt + 1 else: return Failure(error)$
$backoff(attempt: ℕ, strategy: BackoffStrategy) → Time = match strategy with | Exponential(base) → base × 2^{attempt} | Linear(interval) → interval × attempt | Fixed(delay) → delay$
fx: Calculates wait time based on strategy

ops.def: $retryable(error: Error) → 𝔹 = error.type ∈ {NetworkTimeout, ServiceUnavailable, RateLimited}$
fx: Classifies error as retryable or not

name: Unit Test Pattern
desc: Circuit breaker pattern prevents cascading failures by stopping requests to failing services.

props.spec: Each test verifies one behavior Clear failure messages

ops.sig: bdd_test(given: Setup, when: Action, then: Assertion) → TestResult
ops.def: $bdd\_test(given: Setup, when: Action, then: Assertion) → TestResult = state := given(); result := when(state); then(result)$
pre:
given 
eq null \wedge when 
eq null \wedge then 
eq null
fx: Structures test in readable BDD format

ops.sig: table_test(cases: Array⟨TestCase⟩, test_fn: TestCase → TestResult) → Array⟨TestResult⟩
ops.def: $table\_test(cases: Array⟨TestCase⟩, test\_fn: TestCase → TestResult) → Array⟨TestResult⟩ = map(test\_fn, cases)$
fx: Runs same test with multiple inputs

ops.sig: param_test(params: Array⟨Param⟩, test: Param → TestResult) → Array⟨TestResult⟩
ops.def: $param\_test(params: Array⟨Param⟩, test: Param → TestResult) → Array⟨TestResult⟩ = map(test, params)$
$execute_test(test: Test) → TestResult = context := test.arrange() result := test.act(context) passed := test.assert(result) test.cleanup(context) return TestResult(passed, result, context)$
$run_suite(tests: Set⟨Test⟩) → SuiteResult = results := [] for test in tests: result := execute_test(test) results := results ∪ {result} return SuiteResult(results, summary(results))$

$mock(dependency: Dependency, behavior: Specification) → Mock = mock := Mock(dependency) configure(mock, behavior) return mock test_addition() → 𝔹 // Arrange: Set up test data = calculator := Calculator() a := 2 b := 3 expected := 5 // Act: Exercise the unit result := calculator.add(a, b) // Assert: Verify expectations assert(result = expected, "2 + 3 should equal 5") // Cleanup (if needed) cleanup() Mock dependency behavior: mock_database := Mock(Database) mock_database.when(get_user(1)).then_return(User(1, "Alice")) service := UserService(mock_database) user := service.get_user(1) assert(user.name = "Alice") verify(mock_database.get_user).called_once_with(1)$

$call(operation: Operation) → Result = match state with | Closed → try: result := operation(); record\_success(); return Success(result) catch error: record\_failure(); if failure\_threshold\_exceeded(): open(); return Failure(error) | Open → if timeout\_elapsed(): transition\_half\_open(); return CircuitOpen | HalfOpen → try: result := operation(); close(); return Success(result) catch error: open(); return Failure(error)$
post: result = Success(v) ∨ result = Failure(e) ∨ result = CircuitOpen

ops.def: $open() → Effect = state := Open; open\_timestamp := now()$
$reset() → Effect = state := Closed; failure\_count := 0$

name: Integration Test Pattern
desc: Bulkhead pattern isolates resources to prevent cascade failures across system components.

ops.def: $test\_top\_down(module: Module, deps: Set⟨Module⟩) → TestResult = stub\_dependencies(deps); test\_module(module); verify\_integration()$
fx: Records integration points between module and deps

ops.def: $test\_bottom\_up(modules: Sequence⟨Module⟩) → TestResult = for module in reverse\_dependency\_order(modules): test(module); integrate\_with\_tested()$
fx: Builds confidence in foundation before integration

ops.def: $test\_big\_bang(system: System) → TestResult = integrate\_all\_modules(); test\_entire\_system()$

ops.sig: test_sandwich(top: Set⟨Module⟩, middle: Set⟨Module⟩, bottom: Set⟨Module⟩) → TestResult
ops.def: $test\_sandwich(top: Set⟨Module⟩, middle: Set⟨Module⟩, bottom: Set⟨Module⟩) → TestResult = test\_top\_down(top); test\_bottom\_up(bottom); integrate\_middle(middle)$
fx: Stubs middle layer to isolate integration issues

ops.def: $mock\_integration(interface: Interface, behavior: Behavior) → Mock = create\_mock(interface); configure\_behavior(behavior); return\_mock()$
$mock(interface: Interface) → Mock = create\_mock(interface); configure\_expectations(); return\_mock\_instance()$
$stub(method: Method, return\_value: Value) → Stub = stub\_method := λ args: return\_value$
fx: Isolates system under test from dependencies

ops.def: $spy(object: Object) → Spy = wrap(object); record\_calls(); forward\_to\_real\_object()$
fx: Wraps real object to record interactions
Preserves original behavior while tracking calls

ops.def: $fake(interface: Interface, simple\_impl: Implementation) → Fake = create\_working\_implementation(simple\_impl)$
fx: Faster than real implementation for testing

ops.def: $fixture(setup: () → State) → State = setup()$
$setup_environment() → Environment = db := create_test_database() services := start_test_services() config := load_test_config() return Environment(db, services, config)$
$execute_integration(test: IntegrationTest, env: Environment) → TestResult = setup_data(env.database) result := test.run(env) verify_state(env) return result$

$teardown(env: Environment) → Effect = env.database.drop() env.services.stop() cleanup_test_data() integration_test_user_registration() // Setup environment = test_db := create_test_database() api_client := create_api_client() email_service := create_mock_email_service() // Execute integration flow response := api_client.post("/register", { username: "newuser", email: "new@example.com", password: "secure123" }) // Verify database state user := test_db.query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = ?", ["newuser"]) assert(user ≠ null) assert(user.email = "new@example.com") // Verify email sent assert(email_service.sent_count = 1) assert(email_service.last_email.to = "new@example.com") // Cleanup test_db.drop()$

ops.sig: allocate(pool: ResourcePool, request: Request) → Resource | null
ops.def: $allocate(pool: ResourcePool, request: Request) → Resource | null = if pool.available > 0: resource := pool.resources.take(); pool.available := pool.available - 1; return resource else: return null$
$release(pool: ResourcePool, resource: Resource) → Effect = pool.resources.return(resource); pool.available := pool.available + 1$
$create(name: String, size: ℕ) → ResourcePool = resources := create\_resources(size); pool := ResourcePool(name, resources, available=size); return pool$

name: End-to-End Test Pattern
desc: Graceful degradation pattern maintains partial functionality when components fail.

types.def: CSS(selector: String) | XPath(expression: String) | ID(id: String)

ops.def: $log(level: LogLevel, message: String) → Effect = if level >= min\_level: write\_log(timestamp(), level, message)$

ops.sig: log_structured(level: LogLevel, message: String, context: Map) → Effect
ops.def: $log\_structured(level: LogLevel, message: String, context: Map) → Effect = entry := {time: now(), level, message, context}; write\_json(entry)$
$launch_browser(config: BrowserConfig) → Browser = driver := create_webdriver(config.type, config.capabilities) browser := Browser(driver, config) return browser$

$execute_scenario(scenario: UserStory, browser: Browser) → TestResult = pages := load_page_objects(browser) for step in scenario.steps: execute_step(step, pages, browser) results := verify_assertions(scenario.assertions) return TestResult(results)$

Use Page Objects: Encapsulate page structure Reusable across tests Easier maintenance 2. Explicit Waits: Wait for specific conditions Don't use sleep() Handle async behavior 3. Independent Tests: Each test starts from clean state No dependencies between tests Can run in any order 4. Minimize Test Data: Use minimum data needed Clean up after test Avoid shared test data 5. props.d: All credentials are encrypted at rest

ops.def: $store(name: String, credential: Credential) → Effect = encrypted := encrypt(credential, master\_key); vault[name] := encrypted; audit\_log("credential\_stored", name)$

$retrieve(name: String) → Credential | Error = if name ∈ vault: encrypted := vault[name]; credential := decrypt(encrypted, master\_key); audit\_log("credential\_retrieved", name); return credential else: throw NotFoundError(name)$
$rotate(name: String, new\_credential: Credential) → Effect = old := retrieve(name); store(name, new\_credential); archive(name, old); notify\_dependent\_systems(name)$
store(service, cred) encrypts and stores credential
retrieve(service, auth) returns credential if authorized
rotate(service) generates and stores new credential

manif.d: Applied in ai, ui systems and architectures

name: Trust Score Indicator
desc: Trust score indicators display reliability and trustworthiness metrics for data sources and agents.

props.d: Different trust levels are visually distinct

ops.def: $compute(source: Source, history: History) → TrustScore = accuracy := compute\_accuracy(history); reliability := compute\_reliability(history); recency := compute\_recency(history); score := weighted\_average(accuracy, reliability, recency); return TrustScore(score, factors={accuracy, reliability, recency})$
$display(score: TrustScore) → Indicator = level := categorize(score.score); color := get\_color(level); icon := get\_icon(level); return Indicator(level, color, icon)$

$explain(score: TrustScore) → Explanation = factor\_explanations := {explain\_factor(f) : f ∈ score.factors}; recommendations := generate\_improvement\_suggestions(score); return Explanation(score.score, factor\_explanations, recommendations)$
compute() calculates trust score from factors and history
factors() returns contributing trust factors
update(interaction) appends to interaction history

name: Graph Database
desc: Graph databases store data as nodes and edges optimized for traversing relationships.

types.def: (id: EdgeID, from: Node, to: Node, type: EdgeType, properties: Properties)
Sequence of connected nodes and edges

ops.def: $add\_node(label: Label, properties: Properties) → Node = node := Node(id=generate\_id(), label, properties); graph.nodes := graph.nodes ∪ {node}; return node$

ops.sig: add_edge(from: Node, to: Node, type: EdgeType, properties: Properties) → Edge
ops.def: $add\_edge(from: Node, to: Node, type: EdgeType, properties: Properties) → Edge = edge := Edge(id=generate\_id(), from, to, type, properties); graph.edges := graph.edges ∪ {edge}; return edge$

ops.sig: traverse(start: Node, pattern: Pattern, depth: ℕ) → Set⟨Path⟩
ops.def: $traverse(start: Node, pattern: Pattern, depth: ℕ) → Set⟨Path⟩ = paths := ∅; visited := ∅; explore(start, pattern, depth, [], visited, paths); return paths$

ops.sig: match(input: String, pattern: Pattern) → Match | null
ops.def: $match(input: String, pattern: Pattern) → Match | null = if pattern.test(input): return Match(groups, start, end) else: return null$

ops.sig: shortest_path(start: Node, end: Node) → Path | null
ops.def: $shortest\_path(start: Node, end: Node) → Path | null = dijkstra(graph, start, end)$

name: Document Store
desc: Document stores save and retrieve semi-structured documents with flexible schemas.

types.def: String | ℕ | ℝ | 𝔹 | null | Sequence⟨Value⟩ | Map⟨String,Value⟩

props.d: Queries handle nested and array fields

ops.def: $insert(collection: Collection, doc: Document) → DocumentID = id := generate\_id(); doc.id := id; validate\_document(doc); collections[collection].docs := collections[collection].docs ∪ {doc}; update\_indexes(collection, doc); return id$
$find(collection: Collection, query: Query) → Set⟨Document⟩ = {doc ∈ collections[collection].docs : matches(doc, query)}$

ops.sig: update(collection: Collection, id: DocumentID, updates: Updates) → Effect
ops.def: $update(collection: Collection, id: DocumentID, updates: Updates) → Effect = doc := find\_by\_id(collection, id); apply\_updates(doc, updates); validate\_document(doc); update\_indexes(collection, doc)$
find({age: {$gt: 25}, city: "NYC"}) → Set⟨Document⟩
insert(collection, document) → Effect
update(query, changes) → Effect

name: Time-Series Store
desc: Time-series stores optimize storage and retrieval of timestamped data points for analytics.

props.d: Data is stored in time order

ops.def: $write(point: DataPoint) → Effect = validate\_timestamp(point.timestamp); buffer.append(point); if buffer.full(): flush\_to\_disk(); update\_indexes(point)$

ops.sig: query(metric: Metric, start: Time, end: Time) → Sequence⟨DataPoint⟩
ops.def: $query(metric: Metric, start: Time, end: Time) → Sequence⟨DataPoint⟩ = index\_scan := find\_blocks(metric, start, end); points := {p ∈ blocks : p.metric = metric ∧ start ≤ p.timestamp ≤ end}; return sort\_by\_time(points)$
fx: Uses time index for fast lookup

ops.sig: aggregate(metric: Metric, start: Time, end: Time, func: AggregateFunc) → ℝ
ops.def: $aggregate(metric: Metric, start: Time, end: Time, func: AggregateFunc) → ℝ = points := query(metric, start, end); values := {p.value : p ∈ points}; return func(values)$
$range(t1: Time, t2: Time) → TimeSeries = {(t, v) ∈ series : t1 ≤ t ≤ t2}$

name: Event Bus
desc: Event buses enable decoupled pub-sub communication between system components.

types.def: (type: String, payload: Map⟨String, Value⟩, timestamp: Time)

ops.def: $publish(topic: Topic, event: Event) → Effect = subscribers := get\_subscribers(topic); for sub in subscribers: dispatch(sub, event)$
$subscribe(topic: Topic, handler: EventHandler) → Subscription = sub := Subscription(topic, handler, id=generate\_id()); subscriptions[topic] := subscriptions[topic] ∪ {sub}; return sub$
$unsubscribe(subscription: Subscription) → Effect = subscriptions[subscription.topic] := subscriptions[subscription.topic] \ {subscription}$
$send\_at\_most\_once(msg: Message) → Effect = send(msg); // No retry, no ack$
$send\_at\_least\_once(msg: Message) → Effect = send(msg); wait\_for\_ack(); if timeout: retry(msg)$
$send\_exactly\_once(msg: Message) → Effect = if not sent(msg.id): send(msg); mark\_sent(msg.id); dedup\_on\_receive(msg.id)$

name: Real-Time Sync
desc: Real-time sync keeps data synchronized across multiple clients with conflict resolution.

types.def: (type: ChangeType, data: Data, timestamp: Time, author: Client)

props.d: Clients eventually converge to same state

ops.def: $broadcast(change: Change, clients: Set⟨Client⟩) → Effect = for client in clients: send(client, change); track\_acknowledgment(client, change)$
$merge(local: State, remote: Change) → State = if conflicts(local, remote): resolved := resolve\_conflict(local, remote); apply(local, resolved) else: apply(local, remote)$

$sync(client: Client) → Effect = local\_changes := get\_pending\_changes(client); server\_changes := get\_server\_changes\_since(client.last\_sync); send(client, server\_changes); receive(local\_changes); client.last\_sync := now()$
$poll(endpoint: URL, interval: Time) → Stream⟨Update⟩ = every(interval): data := fetch(endpoint); if changed(data): emit(data)$
$websocket(url: URL) → Stream⟨Message⟩ = conn := connect(url); on\_message(msg): emit(msg)$
$transform(op1: Operation, op2: Operation) → Operation = adjust op1 for concurrent op2$
$diff(old: State, new: State) → Delta = changes := [] for path in keys(old) ∪ keys(new): if old[path] ≠ new[path]: changes := changes ++ [Change(path, old[path], new[path])] return changes$

$merge(state: State, delta: Delta) → State = for change in delta: if ¬conflict(change, state): state[change.path] := change.new_value else: conflicts := conflicts ++ [Conflict(change.path, state[change.path], change.new_value)] return state$

$resolve(conflict: Conflict, strategy: Strategy) → Value = case strategy of LastWriteWins → newer(conflict.local_value, conflict.remote_value) ManualResolve → prompt_user(conflict) MergeValues → merge_function(conflict.local_value, conflict.remote_value)$

name: Request-Response API
desc: Request-response APIs provide synchronous communication with clients waiting for responses.

types.def: (method: HTTP_Method, endpoint: Endpoint, params: Parameters, headers: Headers)

ops.sig: request(endpoint: Endpoint, params: Parameters) → Response | Error
ops.def: $request(endpoint: Endpoint, params: Parameters) → Response | Error = validate\_params(params); http\_request := build\_request(endpoint, params); response := http\_client.send(http\_request); return parse\_response(response)$
$handle(request: Request) → Response = authenticate(request); authorize(request); validate(request.params); result := process(request); format\_response(result)$

ops.sig: cache(request: Request, response: Response, ttl: Time) → Effect
ops.def: $cache(request: Request, response: Response, ttl: Time) → Effect = key := hash(request); cache[key] := (response, expires=now()+ttl)$
$register(endpoint: Endpoint, handler: Handler) → Effect = handlers[endpoint] := handler$

$add_middleware(mw: Middleware) → Effect = middleware := middleware ++ [mw] - Authentication: Verify credentials - Logging: Log request/response - Rate limiting: Throttle requests - CORS: Handle cross-origin requests - Compression: Gzip responses$

name: Streaming Protocol
desc: Streaming protocols enable continuous data flow between client and server with backpressure.

types.def: (generate: () → Chunk | EndOfStream, pause: () → Effect, resume: () → Effect)

props.spec: ∀d1,d2: write(d1) before write(d2) ⇒ read(d1) before read(d2)

ops.def: $open(endpoint: Endpoint) → Stream = connection := establish\_connection(endpoint); stream := Stream(connection, buffer=create\_buffer()); register\_handlers(stream); return stream$
$write(stream: Stream, data: Data) → Effect = if stream.buffer.full(): apply\_backpressure(); stream.buffer.enqueue(data); flush\_when\_ready()$
$read(stream: Stream) → Data | EOF = if stream.buffer.empty(): wait\_for\_data(); if stream.closed(): return EOF else: return stream.buffer.dequeue()$
$read(stream: ReadableStream) → Promise⟨Chunk⟩ = reader := stream.getReader(); reader.read()$
$write(stream: WritableStream, chunk: Chunk) → Promise⟨void⟩ = writer := stream.getWriter(); writer.write(chunk)$

ops.sig: transform(input: ReadableStream, f: Chunk → Chunk) → ReadableStream
ops.def: $transform(input: ReadableStream, f: Chunk → Chunk) → ReadableStream = pipe(input, map(f))$
$duplex() → (ReadableStream, WritableStream) = create bidirectional stream pair$

$consume() → Effect = while ¬empty(buffer): chunk := dequeue(buffer) consumer.consume(chunk)
    if buffer.size < lowWaterMark
    ∧ state = paused: state := flowing producer.resume()$

name: Status Bar/Indicator
desc: Status bars and indicators display system state and notifications in a persistent UI element.

ops.def: $update(key: String, value: String) → Effect = status\_items[key] := value; refresh\_display()$
$add(indicator: Indicator, position: Position) → Effect = indicators[position] := indicator; relayout(); refresh\_display()$
$clear(key: String) → Effect = status\_items := status\_items \ {key}; refresh\_display()$
$\text{process}(i) = o$ where $o$ is computed result
$\text{display}(o)$ shows output to user
$\text{update}(s)$ refreshes display

name: Toast/Notification
desc: Toasts and notifications display temporary messages to inform users of events and updates.

types.def: (id: ToastID, message: String, type: ToastType, duration: Time)

ops.sig: show(message: String, type: ToastType, duration: Time) → Toast
ops.def: $show(message: String, type: ToastType, duration: Time) → Toast = toast := Toast(message, type, id=generate\_id()); display(toast); schedule\_dismiss(toast, duration); return toast$
$dismiss(toast\_id: ToastID) → Effect = toast := toasts[toast\_id]; animate\_out(toast); remove(toasts, toast\_id)$
$queue(message: String, type: ToastType) → Effect = if |active\_toasts| < max\_concurrent: show(message, type, default\_duration) else: toast\_queue.enqueue((message, type))$
$\text{process}(i) = o$ where $o$ is computed result
$\text{display}(o)$ shows output to user
$\text{update}(s)$ refreshes display

name: Progress Indicator
desc: Progress indicators show completion status of long-running operations.

ops.def: $start(task: Task, total: ℕ) → ProgressBar = progress := ProgressBar(task, total, current=0); display(progress); return progress$
$update(progress: ProgressBar, current: ℕ) → Effect = progress.current := current; percentage := current / progress.total; refresh\_display(progress, percentage)$
$complete(progress: ProgressBar) → Effect = update(progress, progress.total); animate\_completion(); schedule\_hide(progress, delay)$
$\text{reset}(P) = P'$where$P'.current = 0$,$P'.state = \text{running}$,$P'.eta = \text{estimate}()$

$\text{estimateETA}(P) = \begin{cases} 0 & \text{if } state = \text{complete} \\ \frac{total - current}{rate} & \text{if } rate > 0 \\ \infty & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$where$rate = \frac{current}{elapsed}$
